name: Spawn Player
desc: Spawns another player entity in the world with UUID, position (fixed-point int × 32), rotation (byte × 256/360), held item, and metadata.
notes: This is received when another player enters the client's render distance. ESP modules add the entity to their target list. AntiBot modules check the UUID against tab list entries.

implementation.overview: S0CPacketSpawnPlayer creates a new EntityOtherPlayerMP in the world. It carries the player's entity ID, UUID, initial position (as fixed-point ints), rotation, held item, and metadata watcher. This is the primary trigger for ESP/NameTags modules (add player to tracked list), AntiBot (validate UUID against tab list), and Target tracking (update KillAura target list with new player).

modules.name: ESP / NameTags / Player Tracker
modules.purpose: Detect when a new player enters render distance and add them to the ESP overlay, KillAura target list, or name tag display.
modules.how_it_works: When S0C arrives, the module extracts entityId and UUID. It adds the entity to a tracked list for ESP rendering (box, tracer, health bar, nametag). For AntiBot, the UUID is checked against S38PacketPlayerListItem tab entries. If no tab entry exists for this UUID, the entity is flagged as a bot and skipped.
modules.vanilla_hook: NetHandlerPlayClient.handleSpawnPlayer(S0CPacketSpawnPlayer) — creates EntityOtherPlayerMP, adds it to WorldClient. Fires through PacketReceiveEvent in all hacked clients.
modules.anti_cheat_notes: AntiBot modules use S0C + S38PlayerListItem to filter bots. Some anti-cheats (Watchdog, Grim) spawn bot players that also get proper S0C packets but don't have S38 entries, or have fake S38 entries with default Steve skins.

implementation.general_hooks:
Client: NetHandlerPlayClient.handleSpawnPlayer(S0CPacketSpawnPlayer) — adds other player to world. Hacked clients fire PacketReceiveEvent before this handler.

Server: EntityTrackerEntry sends S0C to players within tracking range when a new player enters the area.
implementation.client_variations:
Rise 6.0: ESP adds player from S0C. AntiBot validates UUID against S38.
Sigma 4.11: Killaura.targetsFromS0C. ESP render list.
RektSky B5: No specific S0C module.
Tenacity 6.0: ESP adds to render list.
Aqua 13: EventPacket fires.
Jigsaw 0.26: No specific S0C module.
